_purpose: Per-file-class pre-edit checklists, surfaced by rule-file-self-audit-gate.py PostToolUse hook after each Edit/Write on rule-class files.
_order_note: More specific patterns MUST come before more generic ones; the hook walks this list top-to-bottom and uses the first match.

checklist: Did you grep universal-protocols.md for related rule names + topic keywords BEFORE this edit?
Did you classify the relationship of the new content (new / subsumed / supersedes / contradicts) against every existing match?
If contradicts: did you STOP and surface to the user rather than write the new entry unilaterally?
If supersedes: did you mark the existing entry superseded with date + reason in the same change?
Did you record the relationship classification in the entry itself OR in the commit message?
Did you grep lessons-learned.md for related categories (Preferences, Process Decisions, Architecture Direction, Errors, etc.) BEFORE this edit?
Did you check whether this lesson contradicts an existing entry on the same topic?
If contradicts: did you decide explicitly which entry stays (and mark the other superseded)?
Did you pick the correct category (the 7 documented categories), not invent a new one?
Did you backup the file to a timestamped .bak BEFORE the write?
Did you verify allow ∩ deny == 0 after the edit (no path appears in BOTH lists)?
Did you justify each new allow/deny entry with a documented reason?
Did you check for orphan deny rules that no current protocol justifies?
Did you use Bash + Python open() per universal-protocols.md 'Modifying ~/.claude/settings.json' (NOT Edit / Write / sed / cmd)?
Did you empirically test ONE path the change was supposed to enable / block?
Did you set frontmatter required fields (per ~/.claude/config/frontmatter-schemas.json k1-sot class)?
If status=SUPERSEDED, did you set superseded_by + superseded_date + superseded_reason?
Did you bump version on substantive change (Rule 1 of Document Versioning Protocol)?
Did you update previous_versions narrative?
Did you verify the K1 SoT path matches the workspace scope-ownership map?
Did you confirm this companion is POINTER (headers + bullets + citations) and not canonical prose?
Did you set status + last_rebuilt + reason in frontmatter?
Did you cite K1 SoT paths with version pins per SoT-Reference Discipline?
If superseding a prior version, did you set superseded_by?
Did you set frontmatter status + created + last_updated?
Did you update plans-registry.md with this plan's row?
Does this plan reference its trigger WR or spec by id?
Did you set date + session_context + status + relates_to + priority + ai_preliminary_thoughts?
If cross-workspace topic, did you add routed_to and file routed-task copies per Brain Dump Capture Protocol?
Did you classify the entry (Active / Completed / Tabled / Abandoned)?
Did you check for duplicate plan entries (same plan listed twice, or two plans for the same work)?
Did you cross-reference the plan file's status header against this registry row?
If completing/abandoning: did you move the row from Active to Completed AND archive the plan file?
Did you check the 200-line cap on MEMORY.md (content beyond line 200 is silently truncated and invisible)?
Did you check sibling topic files in the same memory directory for content overlap?
Is detailed content in the FIRST 150 lines (most-important-first discipline)?
Does each Resume Instruction reference a topic file rather than holding the detail inline?
Did you check sibling topic files in the same directory for overlap or contradiction?
Did you use the right type prefix (feedback_ / decision_ / project_ / reference_ / session_)?
Is the topic narrow enough that it belongs in a separate file (instead of inline in MEMORY.md)?
Did you update MEMORY.md to reference this topic file (or confirm it already does)?
Did you check that the same content isn't already in universal-protocols.md (workspace CLAUDE.md should REFERENCE universal protocols, not DUPLICATE them)?
Did you check sibling workspaces' CLAUDE.md for cross-workspace consistency (HQ / Skill Hub / Sentinel)?
If this content is a universal rule rather than workspace-specific: should it move to universal-protocols.md instead?
Did you verify the workspace-specific addition does not contradict universal-protocols.md (local override is allowed but must be explicit)?
Did you grep this rule file for related topic keywords BEFORE the edit?
Did you check the relationship against universal-protocols.md (is this rule already covered there?)?
Did you classify (new / subsumed / supersedes / contradicts) any existing material on the same topic?
If the rule is universal across workspaces: should it live in universal-protocols.md instead of a separate rule file?

categories.A: Explicit user direction — 'skip X' or equivalent literal phrase in CURRENT session's user message
categories.B: Emergency manual repair — brain row missing / drift correction / production-down fix where the gate would block a fix-in-progress
categories.C: Self-referential meta-edit — the edit IS to the gate's own infrastructure (e.g., editing rule-file-self-audit-gate.py itself or this checklist config)
categories.D: Standing exemption — documented in this gate's own definition or in universal-protocols.md (must cite the source)

rejected_excuses: We already ran this scan earlier in the session
It would be faster to do it from here
